channel.title: 2026-06 on Sujith Quintelier
channel.description: Recent content in 2026-06 on Sujith Quintelier

item.title: github: Track total merges by adoption phase in enterprise and organization reports
item.description:
GitHub enterprise and organization reports now include the total number of pull requests merged by each AI adoption phase cohort. This extends the adoption phase metrics previously added to the Copilot usage metrics API.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: k8s: Open source maintainership in the age of AI
item.description:
Kubernetes says AI-assisted contributions are welcome, but maintainers must keep human accountability, transparency, and verification at the center. The community is also testing AI review tools such as Copilot and CodeRabbit to help with pull request review and maintainer workload.

  • Source: Kubernetes Official Blog

item.title: github: MAI-Code-1-Flash for Copilot Business and Copilot Enterprise
item.description:
MAI-Code-1-Flash, Microsoft AI’s in-house coding model, is now generally available for GitHub Copilot Business and Copilot Enterprise. It builds on its recent expansion across Copilot surfaces and is described as purpose-built for coding and optimized.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Desktop 3.6: Worktrees and deeper Copilot integration
item.description:
GitHub Desktop 3.6 adds Git worktree support and expands GitHub Copilot integration for commit authoring and merge conflict resolution. It brings more of the Git workflow into Desktop.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: aks: Release - 2026-06-19
item.description:
AKS 2026-06-19 adds Kubernetes 1.36 GA/LTS, Application Gateway for Containers GA, and several platform updates including FIPS on Ubuntu 22.04, CVM with Azure Linux GA, and multiple CSI/CNI/component patch upgrades. It also changes some defaults and validations, including Istio CNI behavior for new asm-1-30 installs, dual-stack IPv6 CIDR derivation, and stricter checks for gMSA and FIPS/Kata combinations.

  • Source: Release notes from AKS

item.title: security: Photo ZIP campaign targeting hospitality industry delivers Node.js implant for persistent access
item.description:
Microsoft Threat Intelligence reported an active multi-stage intrusion campaign targeting hospitality organizations in Europe and Asia. The attackers use photo-themed ZIP archives and fake image shortcut files to deploy a persistent Node.js implant and avoid detection.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: k8s: Introducing the Cluster API plugin for Headlamp
item.description:
Headlamp now has an alpha Cluster API plugin that adds visual management and debugging views for CAPI resources inside the UI. It covers cluster health, resource hierarchies, scaling, bootstrap config inspection, topology awareness, map view, and inline Prometheus metrics support.

  • Source: Kubernetes Official Blog

item.title: github: Copilot code review: Analysis depth and efficiency updates
item.description:
Copilot code review now uses built-in file exploration tools from the Copilot CLI and SDK. GitHub says this improves review cost efficiency without changing the existing workflow.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Enterprise-managed settings now support strictKnownMarketplaces in VS Code and GitHub Copilot CLI
item.description:
GitHub added enterprise-managed support for strictKnownMarketplaces in VS Code and GitHub Copilot CLI. The setting is in public preview and lets enterprises control which plugins users can install.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: k8s: Inspect Volcano workloads faster with Headlamp
item.description:
The Volcano plugin for Headlamp adds dedicated views for Volcano Jobs, Queues, and PodGroups, plus a map view that shows how they relate to Pods and events. It is meant to improve interactive troubleshooting and inspection of batch workloads without replacing kubectl or the Volcano CLI.

  • Source: Kubernetes Official Blog

item.title: github: Saved views for repository issues – Public Preview and adjustable row heights in projects
item.description:
GitHub added saved views to repository Issues pages in public preview, allowing teams to create and share filtered issue views. The release also includes adjustable row heights in Projects.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: More control over your GitHub-hosted runners
item.description:
GitHub Actions org admins can now control access to GitHub-hosted runners more tightly. They can disable standard hosted-runner labels like ubuntu-latest and add custom labels for finer-grained usage control.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: k8s: See your serverless: introducing the Headlamp plugin for Knative
item.description:
Headlamp now has a Knative plugin that adds integrated views and actions for Knative resources in the Kubernetes UI. It centralizes KService management, traffic splitting, autoscaling configuration, metrics, and related CRDs, with release 0.3.0-beta available.

  • Source: Kubernetes Official Blog

item.title: github: Actions steps can now be run in parallel
item.description:
GitHub Actions now supports running workflow steps concurrently by running them in the background. Previously, steps always executed sequentially, with each step waiting for the prior one to finish.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: npm adds preventive account protection for high-impact accounts
item.description:
npm now applies a temporary preventive safeguard to high-impact accounts when it detects sensitive account changes. The measure is intended to reduce account-takeover risk for maintainers of widely used packages.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: Retirement: Migrate from Azure Blueprints by January 31, 2027
item.description:
Azure Blueprints retirement has been extended from July 11, 2026 to January 31, 2027. Phased retirement starts July 31, 2026, when creating new blueprint definitions will no longer be supported.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: security: Microsoft a Leader in The Forrester Wave™ for Endpoint Management Platforms
item.description:
Microsoft says it was named a Leader in Forrester Wave: Endpoint Management Platforms, Q2 2026, and reports the highest scores in the current offering and strategy categories.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: github: Red Hat Enterprise Linux runner images are now in public preview
item.description:
GitHub-hosted larger runners now support Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 and 10 images in public preview. The images were introduced in partnership with Red Hat and are available for organizations to use.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot for Jira is now generally available
item.description:
GitHub Copilot for Jira is now generally available after a public preview that began in March 2026. GitHub says it has added several improvements since preview, including model selection.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Cost centers now support enterprise teams
item.description:
GitHub cost centers now support adding enterprise teams as resources, so usage can be attributed according to your team structure. This update extends cost attribution for enterprises beyond individual resources.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Application Gateway for Containers – Inference gateway
item.description:
Application Gateway for Containers is adding a preview inference gateway capability. It brings the Kubernetes Gateway API Inference Extension to support AI inference traffic handling for Kubernetes workloads.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: k8s: Spotlight on WG Device Management
item.description:
The Device Management WG is working on Kubernetes support for GPUs, TPUs, NICs, and other specialized hardware through Dynamic Resource Allocation (DRA), which graduated to GA in Kubernetes 1.34. The group is now expanding DRA and related APIs for sharing, topology-aware scheduling, health monitoring, and multi-node device use.

  • Source: Kubernetes Official Blog

item.title: security: CNAPP evolution: How Microsoft aligns with leading cloud risk management platforms
item.description:
Microsoft’s post says CNAPP platforms are increasingly used to prioritize exploitable risks, reduce exposure, and embed security across the application lifecycle. It positions Microsoft’s approach as aligned with other leading cloud risk management platforms.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: github: Self-service credential revocation for incident response
item.description:
GitHub Enterprise owners can now use a new break-glass capability to immediately revoke all credentials for a compromised account or stolen credentials during incident response. The feature is self-service and intended for urgent security handling.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: security: StealC and Amadey: Breaking down infostealers and the cybercrime services that deliver them
item.description:
Microsoft’s DCU helped disrupt domains supporting the StealC and Amadey infrastructure on June 24, 2026. The post provides a technical overview of the StealC and Amadey malware families and the cybercrime services used to deliver them.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: azure: [In preview] Generally Available: Azure NetApp Files migration assistant
item.description:
Azure NetApp Files migration assistant is in preview and available generally, using SnapMirror for data migration to ANF. It supports migrations from on-premises systems and from CVO or other cloud providers.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: Secret scanning adds extended metadata for Replicate secrets
item.description:
GitHub Secret Scanning now adds extended metadata when it detects Replicate secrets, giving more context about leaked credentials. The change applies to the Replicate secret pattern and is meant to improve incident response and triage.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Fetch Code Quality findings via REST API
item.description:
GitHub has added repository-level REST APIs for Code Quality findings in public preview. The new read-only endpoints expose code quality data via API, bringing parity closer to the GitHub UI.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Automatic Dependabot access to GitHub-hosted registries
item.description:
Dependabot can now read from private GitHub Packages registries without a personal access token when the package has granted repository access through “Manage Actions access.” This removes the need to configure a PAT for that dependency source.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot CLI: New terminal interface is generally available
item.description:
GitHub Copilot CLI’s redesigned terminal interface is now generally available. It adds a tabbed layout for working with GitHub directly from the terminal.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Deprecation of Python 3.9 for Dependabot
item.description:
Dependabot no longer supports Python 3.9 because it has reached end of life. Continuing to use Python 3.9 may prevent Dependabot from creating pull requests.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: devops+ai: 🔥Agent Skills - Towards Autonomous Development, Deployment, and Validation🔥
item.description:
The article is a short note titled “Agent Skills - Towards Autonomous Development, Deployment, and Validation” with the phrase “aka, don’t ask me until prod.” No substantive technical details are provided in the supplied content.

  • Source: Let’s do DevOps

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot app support for BYOK
item.description:
GitHub Copilot app now supports bring your own key (BYOK), allowing agent sessions to use customer-managed model providers. Supported providers include OpenAI, Azure OpenAI, Microsoft Foundry, Anthropic, and more.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: security: Guarding AI memory
item.description:
Microsoft Security Blog discusses risks to AI memory and outlines defenses against attacks that target what an AI system remembers. The post is a high-level security overview rather than a product release note.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: security: One intrusion, two cyberattackers: Uncovering parallel threat activity
item.description:
Microsoft describes a ransomware case where two threat actors operated in parallel during the same intrusion, using overlapping tactics and evasion methods. The report emphasizes that isolated telemetry can miss complex attacks when activity from multiple actors is blended together.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: github: New features and Claude as agent provider preview in JetBrains IDEs
item.description:
GitHub added support for organization and enterprise agents in JetBrains IDEs, along with Copilot CLI message queueing/steering and a new agent debug logs summary view. The update also previews Claude as an agent provider in JetBrains IDEs.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: commandline: Where the agent decides, and where the tools actually run
item.description:
The article frames a design question about where to place an agent’s decision-making logic in an application that already has a deterministic state machine, tool integrations, and CI/CD. It focuses on separating the “brain” (judgment) from the “hands” (tool execution) within a graph/state-based architecture.

  • Source: Microsoft Command Line Blog

item.title: commandline: The durable asset is the loop you own. OpenEnv is its protocol.
item.description:
The article argues that the durable asset in agentic AI is the learning loop, not the rented model, and presents OpenEnv as the protocol for reinforcement learning environments. It positions OpenEnv around owning and standardizing the environment/evaluation loop for recursive self-improvement.

  • Source: Microsoft Command Line Blog

item.title: github: AI credits consumed per user now in the Copilot usage metrics API
item.description:
The Copilot usage metrics API now includes per-user AI credits consumed per day. The new data is derived from the same AI credits consumption source used by the usage-based billing API.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: security: AutoJack: How a single page can RCE the host running your AI agent
item.description:
Microsoft describes AutoJack, an exploit chain where a single malicious webpage can cause remote code execution on the host running an AI browsing agent. The attack abuses localhost trust, missing authentication, and unsafe parameter handling in AutoGen Studio’s MCP WebSocket.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: github: Upcoming deprecation of Opus 4.6 (fast)
item.description:
GitHub will deprecate Opus 4.6 (fast) across all Copilot experiences on June 29, 2026. This includes Copilot Chat, inline edits, ask and agent modes, and code completions.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: ai: Outcome-driven learning systems: Enterprise RL with OpenEnv and Foundry
item.description:
Microsoft says its Build 2026 Foundry releases fit together as an outcome-driven learning system for enterprise reinforcement learning, centered on OpenEnv and Foundry. The post positions hosted agents, Toolboxes, Foundry IQ, Memory, Managed Compute, fine-tuning, Frontier Tuning, and a new evaluation/optimization stack as supporting pieces of that system.

  • Source: Microsoft AI Foundry Blog

item.title: github: MAI-Code-1-Flash available on more Copilot surfaces
item.description:
MAI-Code-1-Flash is now available in additional GitHub Copilot surfaces. The model can be used in Copilot CLI, the GitHub Copilot app, Copilot Chat on GitHub, and Visual Studio Code.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: security: New Forrester study shows customers who unified with Microsoft Security benefited from 124% ROI
item.description:
Microsoft cites a new Forrester Total Economic Impact study saying organizations that consolidated on Microsoft Security saw a 124% ROI, lower risk, and improved readiness to secure AI. The post is a summary announcement and does not include detailed methodology or product changes.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: github: Copilot code review: AGENTS.md support and UI improvements
item.description:
Copilot code review now reads repository-level AGENTS.md files and adds a Request button to make it easier to ask Copilot for a review on draft pull requests. GitHub says these updates are generally available.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Detecting Duplicate Issues – Public Preview and issue fields MCP support for GitHub Issues
item.description:
GitHub Issues adds duplicate issue detection in public preview and expands MCP support for issue fields. The change is aimed at reducing duplicate triage work for maintainers, especially in large repositories.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot-authored pull requests now included in author searches
item.description:
Pull request searches using the author: qualifier now include pull requests opened by the Copilot cloud agent on a user’s behalf. For example, author:@me on GitHub pull requests will return those Copilot-authored PRs as well.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Repository switcher generally available in global navigation
item.description:
GitHub’s repository switcher in global navigation is now generally available. It lets you jump between repositories without leaving the current page.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Actions: Build custom images from custom images
item.description:
GitHub Actions custom images now support building one custom image from another. This adds flexibility for composing and managing image-generation pipelines for GitHub-hosted runners.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Safer pull_request_target defaults for GitHub Actions checkout
item.description:
GitHub is changing the default behavior of the Actions checkout action for workflows triggered by pull_request_target to reduce common security mistakes. The goal is to avoid exposing base-branch credentials or secrets to untrusted pull request code.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Control who and what triggers GitHub Actions workflows
item.description:
GitHub Actions now has workflow execution protections in public preview for GitHub Enterprise, organizations, and repositories. Administrators can define allow lists to control which users or events can trigger workflows.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Generated release notes credit you for Copilot pull requests
item.description:
Generated release notes now credit Copilot pull requests in the list of merged pull requests since the last release. This affects the automatically generated release notes output.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: security: From package to postinstall payload: Inside the Mastra npm supply chain compromise
item.description:
Microsoft describes a compromised npm package in the Mastra ecosystem that spread a hidden postinstall payload to 140+ projects. The post focuses on detection, hunting, and defense guidance using Microsoft Defender and threat intelligence.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: security: Crypto Clipper uses Tor and worm-like propagation for persistence and control
item.description:
Microsoft Threat Intelligence described a cryptocurrency clipper campaign that steals and replaces wallet addresses, uses Tor for command-and-control, and spreads in a worm-like way. The malware also maintains persistence and includes a lightweight backdoor for follow-on activity.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: github: Read remote repository content with GitHub CLI
item.description:
GitHub CLI now includes two commands for reading repository content without cloning: gh repo read-file and gh repo read-dir. These let you inspect remote files and directories directly from the terminal.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: security: Beyond the benchmark: Advancing security at AI speed
item.description:
Microsoft Security says it has advanced its agentic vulnerability detection system, MDASH, and integrated it into workflows across Windows, Azure, and identity systems. The post focuses on applying the system in real-world security operations rather than benchmark results.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: github: Secret scanning updates – June 2026
item.description:
GitHub added secret scanning coverage in June 2026 with new partner integrations, more default push-protection blocks, additional validity checks, and richer metadata for leaked secrets.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: security: ​​Forrester names Microsoft a Leader in the 2026 Extended Detection and Response Platforms Wave™ report
item.description:
Microsoft says Forrester named it a Leader in The Forrester Wave™: Extended Detection and Response Platforms, Q2 2026. The post is an announcement of the analyst recognition and does not include product changes or technical updates.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Databricks natively storing data in Microsoft OneLake
item.description:
Azure Databricks now supports natively writing managed Delta tables to Microsoft OneLake in public preview. This lets customers use OneLake as the storage layer for Databricks workloads without managing separate storage accounts.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Migrate – GitHub Copilot Modernization integration for at scale code assessments
item.description:
Azure Migrate public preview now integrates with GitHub Copilot Modernization to provide at-scale code insights. The integration combines Azure Migrate’s portfolio-level discovery and assessment with Copilot’s context-aware code analysis.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Databricks native read access to Microsoft OneLake
item.description:
Azure Databricks now has generally available native read access to Microsoft OneLake through Unity Catalog. Users can query and analyze OneLake data directly without moving or copying it.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: Enterprise-managed settings now support bypass permission controls
item.description:
Enterprise administrators can now set disableBypassPermissionsMode to "disable" in enterprise-managed settings.json to prevent GitHub Copilot CLI and VS Code from bypassing permission controls.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: dotnet: AI-Powered MSBuild Investigation with the Microsoft Binlog MCP Server
item.description:
Microsoft introduced the Binlog MCP Server for investigating MSBuild build failures and performance issues with AI. It provides 15 specialized tools for analyzing MSBuild binary logs through an AI assistant.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: security: AI is accelerating cyberattacks—here’s how to stay ahead
item.description:
Microsoft describes how its identity and security products use unified signals to help teams prevent, detect, and respond to AI-accelerated attacks faster. The post is a security-focused overview rather than a feature release.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: ICMP Support for Azure Standard V2 NAT Gateway
item.description:
Azure StandardV2 NAT Gateway now supports outbound ICMP Echo Request and Echo Reply traffic. This lets customers use tools like ping to validate outbound connectivity and troubleshoot network issues from workloads behind the gateway.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: Auto mode in Copilot Chat available for all users
item.description:
GitHub Copilot auto model selection is now generally available in Copilot Chat on github.com and the GitHub mobile app for all Copilot plans. In auto mode, Copilot selects the model for the chat request.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Limit open pull requests for users without write access
item.description:
GitHub is adding a limit on how many open pull requests users without write access can have in a repository. The change is intended to reduce repeated low-quality or drive-by PRs and ease triage load for maintainers.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Agent finder for GitHub Copilot now available
item.description:
GitHub Copilot now includes agent finder, a feature for selecting which MCP servers, skills, canvases, agents, and tools an agent should use. GitHub says it reduces the need to manually wire these components and helps avoid filling the context window.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: commandline: Introducing the Agentic Resource Discovery specification
item.description:
Microsoft introduced the Agentic Resource Discovery (ARD) specification, an open standard for AI clients to automatically find resources relevant to a task. It was developed with partners including GoDaddy, Google, and Hugging Face.

  • Source: Microsoft Command Line Blog

item.title: github: Copilot individual plan sign-ups are reopening
item.description:
GitHub is gradually reopening sign-ups for Copilot Individual plans, including Student, Pro, Pro+, and Max, for new subscribers over the next couple of weeks.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot app generally available
item.description:
GitHub Copilot app is now generally available on macOS, Windows, and Linux. It serves as a native desktop app for agent-driven development on GitHub.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Models is no longer available to new customers
item.description:
GitHub is retiring GitHub Models. As an initial step, new customers can no longer use it, while existing organizations or enterprises that have already used it are not affected by this announcement.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: New project templates and template gallery for Azure Functions in VS Code
item.description:
The Azure Functions VS Code extension adds a redesigned Create New Project experience in public preview. It replaces the multi-step Quick Pick wizard with a searchable, filterable visual template gallery.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Log Analytics Summary Rules experience
item.description:
A new Azure portal experience for Summary Rules in Log Analytics is now generally available. Summary Rules aggregate high-volume log data on a defined schedule and store it in summarized tables for faster querying and reporting.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: GitHub Code Quality generally available July 20, 2026
item.description:
GitHub Code Quality, currently in public preview, will reach general availability on July 20, 2026. The preview has been used by enterprises to detect maintainability and reliability issues, enforce quality gates, and track code coverage.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Organization-level enablement for GitHub Code Quality
item.description:
GitHub Code Quality can now be enabled or disabled at the organization level with one toggle. This replaces the need to configure the feature repository by repository.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: commandline: Information-flow control: Moving toward secure, autonomous agents
item.description:
The article says information-flow control (IFC) is a deterministic security approach that could help make autonomous agents more secure. It presents IFC as a path toward controlling how information moves through agent systems.

  • Source: Microsoft Command Line Blog

item.title: github: Copilot usage metrics now include more of your active users
item.description:
GitHub Copilot usage metrics now use server-side telemetry in addition to client-side signals, so reports include more active users. The change affects enterprise usage reports and improves coverage of user activity.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: security: Microsoft Defender email security benchmarking: Key insights from one year of data
item.description:
Microsoft published a blog post summarizing one year of real-world email security benchmarking for Microsoft Defender, comparing its performance against SEG and ICES vendors. The provided excerpt does not include the specific results or metrics.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: k8s: Spotlight on SIG Storage
item.description:
SIG Storage discusses its role in Kubernetes persistent storage, including CSI/COSI, data protection, and emerging work for AI workloads. Recent updates include VolumeGroupSnapshot GA in v1.36, CSI Changed Block Tracking Beta in v1.36, and VolumeAttributesClass GA in v1.34.

  • Source: Kubernetes Official Blog

item.title: github: Copilot code review: New configurations and controls
item.description:
GitHub added new ways to configure Copilot code review, including organization runner controls, support for Copilot content exclusion, and removal of the repository custom instructions character limit.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Actions: Minimum version enforcement timeline for self-hosted runners
item.description:
GitHub Actions is resuming enforcement of minimum version requirements for self-hosted runners on github.com and GitHub Enterprise Cloud with Data Residency. The post says this is part of a broader effort to rebuild enforcement, but the excerpt does not include the specific minimum versions or timeline details.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Enterprise Server 3.21 is now generally available
item.description:
GitHub Enterprise Server 3.21 is now generally available. The release adds improvements in deployment efficiency, monitoring, code security, and policy management, including organization custom properties now generally available.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Bot-created pull requests can run workflows if approved
item.description:
Pull requests created by github-actions[bot] can now run CI/CD workflows after a user approves them. The approval step is required as a security check before generated code is allowed to trigger workflows.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: Retirement: Av2-series, F-series, Fs-series, Fsv2-series, G-series, Gs-series, and Lsv2-series Virtual Machines for Azure Batch pools
item.description:
Azure Compute will retire several VM series used by Azure Batch pools on November 15, 2028. After that date, new Batch pools cannot use these series and existing pools must be moved to supported VM types.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: D-series, Ds-series, Dv2-series, Dsv2-series, and Ls-series Virtual Machines for Azure Batch pools
item.description:
Azure Compute will retire D-series, Ds-series, Dv2-series, Dsv2-series, and Ls-series VMs for Azure Batch pools on May 1, 2028. After that date, new Batch pools cannot be created with these VM series.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: GPv1 and Legacy Blob storage account creation
item.description:
Azure is retiring all General purpose v1 (GPv1) storage accounts, including legacy blob storage accounts. The notice says this is part of ongoing Azure Storage portfolio changes aimed at improving performance, scalability, and cost efficiency.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: SQL MCP Server
item.description:
SQL MCP Server is now generally available. It provides a secure, high-performance way to build agentic solutions with controlled access to production data, and supports SQL with compatibility for PostgreSQL and Azure Cosmos DB.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: AI usage report updates
item.description:
AI usage reports now include GitHub AI Credits usage in the standard report fields. To track AI credit consumption, use the quantity field for AI credit quantity and gross_amount for the associated amount.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot CLI: Configure everything from one place with /settings
item.description:
GitHub Copilot CLI adds a unified /settings command for managing configuration in one place. It consolidates older commands like /theme, /streamer-mode, and /experimental, along with other previously separate options.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: New runner images in public preview
item.description:
GitHub has added two new GitHub-hosted runner images for Actions in public preview. They are available to all users so workflows can be tested on the latest platforms before general release.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: Retirement: Azure Load Balancer Inbound NAT rule version 1 for Azure VMSS (aka Inbound NAT Pools)
item.description:
Microsoft narrowed the scope of an Azure Load Balancer retirement notice. The retirement now applies only to Inbound NAT rules version 1 resources for single VMs, not VMSS NAT pools.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: GitHub Agentic Workflows is now in public preview
item.description:
GitHub Agentic Workflows is now in public preview. It lets you automate reasoning-based tasks such as issue triage, CI failure analysis, and documentation updates using coding agents inside GitHub.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Agentic workflows no longer need a personal access token
item.description:
GitHub Agentic Workflows can now use the built-in GitHub Actions GITHUB_TOKEN instead of requiring a personal access token. This removes the need to create and store a PAT for these workflows.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: List, view, and create discussions in GitHub CLI
item.description:
GitHub CLI now includes a new gh discussion command group for GitHub Discussions. It lets you list, view, create, and update discussions from the CLI.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Manage sub-issues, types, and dependencies from GitHub CLI
item.description:
GitHub CLI now supports managing issue types, parent/sub-issue relationships, and issue dependencies directly from the terminal. This lets you structure and track issues without using the browser.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot Chat now sees your agent sessions
item.description:
GitHub Copilot Chat on the web now has a better handoff to Copilot cloud agent sessions. It also adds the ability to search and query past agent sessions.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Microsoft Entra server principals on Azure SQL Database
item.description:
Microsoft Entra server principals (logins) for Azure SQL Database are now generally available. You can now create logins with CREATE LOGIN ... FROM EXTERNAL PROVIDER in the virtual master database, aligning Microsoft Entra identities with SQL logins.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: dotnet: Join us for .NET Day on Agentic Modernization Livestream
item.description:
Microsoft announced a .NET Day livestream focused on agentic modernization. The post is an event announcement and does not include technical product changes or release details.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: azure: Retirement: Azure Synapse Link for Azure Cosmos DB NoSQL
item.description:
Azure Synapse Link for Azure Cosmos DB NoSQL is being retired. New Azure Cosmos DB NoSQL accounts cannot enable it after March 31, 2026, and existing customers are supported until its end of life on March 31, 2029.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: Azure VPN Client for Linux (Preview) will be retired on August 31, 2026
item.description:
Azure VPN Client for Linux (Preview) will be retired on August 31, 2026 because it has remained in preview and has no path to general availability. The notice says the retirement is part of aligning Azure networking services with current security and reliability standards.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: security: Turn specs into evals for any agent with ASSERT
item.description:
ASSERT is an open-source framework that turns natural-language behavior requirements into executable evaluations for AI models and agents. It is presented as a way to create evaluations and regression tests from specs.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: github: Enterprises can now create up to 500 cost centers
item.description:
GitHub has doubled the enterprise cost center limit from 250 to 500. This applies to enterprises managing many business units, departments, or products.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Incremental analysis for Go, C/C++, and CodeQL CLI
item.description:
CodeQL pull request scans for C/C++ and Go now use incremental analysis, which makes them faster. GitHub says this builds on earlier incremental analysis improvements for other languages and CodeQL CLI.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Dedicated security review command now available in Copilot CLI
item.description:
GitHub Copilot CLI now includes an experimental /security-review slash command for running security reviews on code changes directly in the CLI. The feature is in public preview.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Dependabot version updates now support the Deno ecosystem
item.description:
Dependabot version updates now support Deno. The support is for version updates only, not security updates.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Agent mode for GitHub Copilot in SQL Server Management Studio (SSMS)
item.description:
GitHub Copilot Agent mode is now in public preview in SQL Server Management Studio (SSMS). It can help investigate performance issues, tune queries, review maintenance and configuration, identify security concerns, troubleshoot errors, and assist with operational tasks.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: Upcoming breaking changes for npm v12
item.description:
npm v12 will change the default behavior of npm install with security-related defaults. The post says these changes are already available as warnings in npm 11.16.0 and newer.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: PostgreSQL Hub for Azure Developers
item.description:
PostgreSQL Hub for Azure Developers is now generally available. It provides a centralized set of AI and application development resources for PostgreSQL on Azure, including sample apps, solution accelerators, tutorials, and learning paths.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: dotnet: .NET 11 Preview 5 is now available!
item.description:
The post announces .NET 11 Preview 5 and says it includes updates across the runtime, SDK, libraries, ASP.NET Core, .NET MAUI, C#, Entity Framework Core, and other areas. The provided excerpt does not include specific feature details.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: .NET and .NET Framework June 2026 servicing releases updates
item.description:
This post is a brief recap of the June 2026 servicing updates for .NET and .NET Framework. The provided content does not include specific version numbers, fixes, or security details.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: github: Claude Fable 5 is generally available for GitHub Copilot
item.description:
Claude Fable 5 from Anthropic is now generally available in GitHub Copilot. GitHub describes it as a Mythos-class model aimed at long-horizon, autonomous coding and knowledge-work tasks.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: security: Reconstructing AI activity in investigations
item.description:
Microsoft published a security playbook for investigating AI activity in Microsoft 365 Copilot and Azure AI services. It focuses on using telemetry to reconstruct events, assess data exposure, and speed up threat detection.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: github: Periodic code scanning of inactive repositories
item.description:
GitHub code scanning now supports scheduled security scans for repositories with no pushes or pull requests for six months or longer. This helps organizations keep inactive repositories under periodic review.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure NC RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition v6 Series Virtual Machines
item.description:
Azure NCv6-series virtual machines are now generally available in Southeast Asia and West US 2. They use NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition GPUs with 96 GB of memory per GPU.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Premium SSD v2 disks now support non-zonal Azure Virtual Machines
item.description:
Azure Premium SSD v2 disks are now generally available for non-zonal, single-instance Azure virtual machines in select regions that have Availability Zones. This lets customers deploy Premium SSD v2 without choosing an Availability Zone.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Site Recovery support for Linux Azure VMs with NVMe disk controllers.
item.description:
Azure Site Recovery now supports replication and disaster recovery for Linux Azure VMs with NVMe disk controllers in Azure-to-Azure scenarios. The feature is in public preview and applies to NVMe-enabled Generation 2 VM families such as Da/Ea/Fa v6 and Ebsv5/Ebdsv5.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Microsoft Foundry agent security capabilities in Microsoft Defender for Cloud are transitioning to a Microsoft Agent 365 license
item.description:
Starting July 1, 2026, Microsoft Foundry agent security capabilities in Microsoft Defender for Cloud will move to a Microsoft Agent 365 license. Agent protection in Defender will use Agent 365 observability logs.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Maintenance control for Azure Database for PostgreSQL - reschedule, apply on demand, view and download
item.description:
Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server maintenance control is generally available. The update adds the ability to reschedule maintenance, apply it on demand, and view/download maintenance details.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: dotnet: .NET at Microsoft Build 2026: Must watch sessions
item.description:
Microsoft’s Build 2026 .NET post highlights sessions covering .NET 11, union types in C#, AI building blocks, the agentic web, and .NET MAUI. It is a session roundup rather than a product release note.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Managed Redis now supports Entra ID based RBAC for data management
item.description:
Azure Managed Redis public preview adds Microsoft Entra ID-based RBAC for data access. This lets you control read, write, and admin permissions without using shared keys, using Redis ACLs underneath.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: security: AI brands as bait: How threat actors are using the AI hype in social engineering
item.description:
Threat actors are using AI-related brands and public interest in AI as social engineering lures. Microsoft says attackers are both operationalizing AI to speed up attacks and exploiting AI hype to make scams more convincing.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Ingestion Volume Change dashboard in Metrics Usage Insights
item.description:
Metrics Usage Insights now has an Ingestion Volume Change dashboard in public preview. It lets you compare ingestion volume over time to spot spikes or drops in time series counts and event ingestion rates.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In development] Private Preview: Guest RDMA on Azure Boost
item.description:
Guest RDMA is in private preview on Azure in UK South. It uses Azure Boost to enable RDMA in the guest OS for high-throughput, ultra-low-latency networking within the same region.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: security: Securing CI/CD in an agentic world: Claude Code Github action case
item.description:
Microsoft Threat Intelligence reported a prompt injection path in Claude Code GitHub Action that could expose workflow secrets under specific conditions. The post describes the attack chain, disclosure, Anthropic’s mitigation, and recommendations for securing AI-assisted CI/CD workflows.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: aks: Release 2026-05-29
item.description:
AKS release 2026-05-29 rolls out Kubernetes 1.36 preview and patch updates for 1.35.5/1.34.8/1.33.12, with multiple security and component updates. It also adds Windows Server 2025 and Azure Container Linux GA, expands AKS Automatic and LocalDNS behavior changes, and includes several retirement/deprecation notices for older Windows and Flatcar node images.

  • Source: Release notes from AKS

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Voice sync for avatar support in Voice Live API in Microsoft Foundry
item.description:
Microsoft Foundry Voice Live API now supports avatar voice sync with custom voices in public preview. Developers can pair branded or persona-specific text-to-speech voices with real-time avatar experiences.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Files assessments are now available worldwide using Azure Migrate
item.description:
Azure Migrate now generally supports discovery and assessment of SMB and NFS file shares on Windows and Linux servers worldwide. The update provides a data-driven view of file share environments and helps customers plan migration.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: AI pipelines in Azure HorizonDB
item.description:
Azure HorizonDB AI pipelines are now in public preview. The feature lets you define AI data ingestion workflows in SQL, covering chunking, embedding, extraction, generation, and ranking, and run them as fault-tolerant pipelines inside the database.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Ingest OTLP signals into Azure Monitor with the OpenTelemetry Collector
item.description:
Azure Monitor now generally supports native ingestion of OTLP signals. OpenTelemetry-instrumented applications and platforms can send telemetry directly to Azure Monitor.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: APIM Support for Foundry Models in Azure AI Search
item.description:
Azure API Management now supports all Foundry model integrations in Azure AI Search in public preview. This lets teams put APIM in front of Foundry and Azure OpenAI models used in large-scale RAG pipelines.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure SQL as a knowledge source in Foundry IQ
item.description:
Azure SQL Database is now available in public preview as a first-class Knowledge Source in Azure AI Search / Foundry IQ. This lets developers and architects use authoritative SQL tables as a source for Copilot, RAG, and agentic experiences.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Tool search in Microsoft Foundry toolboxes
item.description:
Microsoft Foundry toolboxes now support tool search in public preview. This helps developers and admins locate tools faster in large catalogs and reduces the cost of sending every tool definition on each turn.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Microsoft Discovery
item.description:
Microsoft Discovery is now generally available. It provides an enterprise platform for building and governing agentic AI workflows for research and development across scientific and engineering disciplines.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Fabric IQ Ontology Knowledge Source in Microsoft Foundry IQ
item.description:
Microsoft Foundry IQ now supports Microsoft Fabric Ontology as a federated knowledge source in public preview. This lets agents query the semantic layer already defined in Fabric using natural language questions.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Microsoft Foundry IQ
item.description:
Microsoft Foundry IQ is now generally available. It provides a managed knowledge layer for grounding agents in enterprise data, with connectors to sources such as SharePoint, OneLake, and Azure Blob.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Private Connectivity for Azure AI Search and Foundry Knowledge Bases
item.description:
Azure AI Search and Foundry Knowledge Bases now support private end-to-end network connectivity between search resources and Foundry services. Traffic for ingestion, enrichment, retrieval, and agent operations can be routed over Shared Private Link or Network Security Perimeter.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: security: Updating the taxonomy of failure modes in agentic AI systems: What a year of red teaming taught us
item.description:
Microsoft Security Blog says a year of red teaming agentic AI systems led to an updated failure-mode taxonomy. The update adds seven new failure modes, including supply chain compromise and goal hijacking, along with recommended mitigations.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Content Understanding chunking and image verbalization in Azure AI Search
item.description:
Azure AI Search public preview adds Content Understanding chunking and image verbalization to the built-in indexing skill. Indexers can now split documents into semantically meaningful chunks and generate text from images during parsing.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Monitor Service Level Indicators (SLI)
item.description:
Azure Monitor now supports Service Level Indicators (SLIs) and Service Level Objectives (SLOs) in general availability. The feature helps teams measure application experience using customer-facing metrics rather than only infrastructure signals.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: commandline: Watch Satya Nadella on a No Priors x Latent Space crossover special
item.description:
A podcast crossover episode featuring Satya Nadella discussing Build announcements and their impact on the developer ecosystem.

  • Source: Microsoft Command Line Blog

item.title: ai: Accelerate Edge AI Development with Foundry Local
item.description:
Microsoft announced Foundry Local for edge AI development, aimed at making it easier to build AI experiences that run inside apps, devices, and other edge systems. The post highlights common production challenges such as model packaging, runtime fragmentation, and hardware differences.

  • Source: Microsoft AI Foundry Blog

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: MAI-Voice-2 in Microsoft Foundry
item.description:
Microsoft Foundry now includes MAI-Voice-2 in public preview. The first-party Microsoft AI voice model supports natural speech in more than 10 languages, voice cloning from a short reference sample, and voice prompting.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: ai: Build 2026: From observability to ROI for AI agents on any framework
item.description:
Microsoft’s Build 2026 post frames AI agent production challenges around accuracy, safety, accountability, and silent drift after deployment. It points to end-to-end observability as the path to managing agents across frameworks and tying operations back to business ROI.

  • Source: Microsoft AI Foundry Blog

item.title: azure: [Launched] General Availability: Backup your ledger files for audit with Azure confidential ledger’s new tool
item.description:
Azure Confidential Ledger introduced a new localized web tool for backing up ledger files so auditors can inspect them and verify cryptographic proofs. The update is in general availability.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: User feedback logging in Microsoft Foundry
item.description:
Microsoft Foundry preview adds end-user feedback logging for AI agents. It lets developers capture structured user feedback such as thumbs up/down, ratings, and custom annotations, and associate that feedback with agent interactions.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Text Analytics for Health NextGen Playground in Azure AI Language
item.description:
The next-generation Text Analytics for Health (TA4H) playground is now generally available in the Microsoft Foundry portal. It lets users paste clinical text such as discharge summaries, doctor’s notes, or research abstracts for analysis.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Self-serve custom photo avatar creation in Microsoft Foundry
item.description:
Microsoft Foundry has generally available self-serve custom photo avatar creation in the Foundry NextGen fine-tuning portal. It is aimed at enterprise customers building voice agents or avatar-and-speech content who need a branded avatar.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Agent-to-agent (A2A) support for Prompt agents and Hosted agents in Foundry
item.description:
Microsoft Foundry Agent Service now supports agent-to-agent (A2A) communication in public preview for Prompt agents and Hosted agents using the responses protocol. Agents can invoke other agents by name through a managed endpoint with identity and content safety controls.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Red Hat OpenShift in Belgium Central
item.description:
Azure Red Hat OpenShift is now generally available in Azure Belgium Central. This adds a new European region for fully managed OpenShift deployments on Azure.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Domain filter for specialized model discovery in Foundry model catalog
item.description:
Microsoft Foundry model catalog now includes a domain filter in public preview. It helps users narrow the catalog of more than 1,900 models by industry or use case.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Evaluations with Intelligent Trace Sampling
item.description:
Microsoft Foundry observability now supports intelligent trace filtering and sampling for evaluations in public preview. It evaluates a representative subset of production traces instead of all traces.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Observability developer experience in Azure Developer CLI (azd)
item.description:
Azure Developer CLI (azd) now has a public preview for observability developer experience, aimed at adding an evaluation loop to agents created with Microsoft Foundry. The preview supports hosted-agent lifecycle workflows in azd, including creating, provisioning, deploying, and initializing evaluation assets.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Rubric evaluator in Microsoft Foundry
item.description:
Microsoft Foundry now offers rubric evaluators in public preview for single-turn and multi-turn agent flows. They let developers define context-specific evaluation criteria with agent prompts for assessing agent quality.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Custom Voice portal experience in Microsoft Foundry
item.description:
Azure AI Speech’s Custom Voice authoring experience is now generally available in the Microsoft Foundry portal. Approved customers can upload voice talent recordings and consent statements, run data quality checks, and train neural voices in the portal.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Microsoft Foundry for Visual Studio Code (June Build 2026 refresh)
item.description:
Microsoft Foundry for Visual Studio Code is now generally available with a Build 2026 refresh. The update adds the full model catalog, model playground, and Hosted agent deployment directly in the editor.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Public Preview: Conversational PII NextGen Playground in Microsoft Foundry
item.description:
Microsoft Foundry has added a public preview Conversational PII NextGen Playground. It accepts transcript input and includes an API Configuration Panel so developers can validate PII detection in multi-speaker dialogue before integration.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Public Preview: TextPII NextGen Playground updates in Microsoft Foundry
item.description:
Microsoft Foundry’s public preview TextPII NextGen Playground has been updated with a refreshed API Configuration Panel and preview features introduced at Ignite 2025. Developers can use it to test predefined PII categories and redaction behavior.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In development] Announcing: Confidential Live Migration for Intel TDX confidential VMs in Azure
item.description:
Microsoft is introducing Confidential Live Migration in Azure for Intel TDX Confidential VMs. The feature is intended to support platform servicing by moving confidential VMs while preserving availability.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Infrastructure Resiliency Manager
item.description:
Azure Infrastructure Resiliency Manager is in public preview. It provides a unified, goal-driven way to design, assess, and improve application resiliency across Azure.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Advanced full-text search in Azure DocumentDB
item.description:
Azure DocumentDB public preview adds advanced full-text search features: fuzzy search, proximity search, expanded language support, and BM25 ranking. It combines these text search capabilities with vector search in a single database.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Database for PostgreSQL - Flexible Server: DuckDB extension
item.description:
Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server now supports installing the DuckDB extension. The update notes that the feature is generally available.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: ai: Expanding the Reach of Document Translation – New Capabilities Announced at Microsoft Build
item.description:
Microsoft announced new Document Translation capabilities in Azure Translator, available in Foundry Tools, with support for translating images, PDFs, Office files, DITA, and XLIFF. The post also mentions future LLM-powered document workflows.

  • Source: Microsoft AI Foundry Blog

item.title: ai: Announcing Foundry Managed Compute: Run open models in Microsoft Foundry
item.description:
Microsoft announced Foundry Managed Compute, a GPU platform-as-a-service for running open-source and custom AI models in Microsoft Foundry. It uses the same endpoint, SDKs, and billing as frontier models.

  • Source: Microsoft AI Foundry Blog

item.title: ai: Introducing Agent Optimizer in Foundry Agent Service
item.description:
Microsoft introduced Agent Optimizer in Foundry Agent Service to help move hosted agents from deployed to production-ready. It targets common issues such as missing required information in conversations before the agent takes action.

  • Source: Microsoft AI Foundry Blog

item.title: ai: Making agent memory more reliable, transparent, and production-ready
item.description:
Microsoft says agent memory is being updated to better support production use, with a focus on reliability, transparency, and consistent task execution. The article frames memory as more than personalization, emphasizing learned procedures, recovery from repeated failures, and improved confidence over time.

  • Source: Microsoft AI Foundry Blog

item.title: ai: Discovery to Execution: Scaling Agents with Toolboxes and Routines in Microsoft Foundry
item.description:
Microsoft Foundry is adding new Toolbox capabilities, including Skills, to help teams discover, manage, and secure tools for production agent workflows. The update is aimed at scaling agents from small-scale use to production execution.

  • Source: Microsoft AI Foundry Blog

item.title: ai: From Building Agents to Working with Them: Enterprise Agent Distribution in Microsoft Foundry
item.description:
Microsoft Foundry shifts focus from agent creation to distribution, emphasizing the enterprise problem of getting AI agents into users’ hands and operating in business workflows. The post frames deployment and adoption of agents as the new bottleneck after rapid progress in agent development.

  • Source: Microsoft AI Foundry Blog

item.title: security: Preinstall to persistence: Inside the Red Hat npm Miasma credential-stealing campaign
item.description:
Microsoft reports a large-scale npm supply chain attack affecting over 90 versions of @redhat-cloud-services packages. The malicious code targets CI/CD and developer systems to steal GitHub, cloud, and local credentials, and propagates by republishing trusted packages.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Simple log alerts in Azure Monitor
item.description:
Azure Monitor’s Simple log alerts are now generally available. The feature adds a simplified way to create log-based alerts for monitoring and alerting.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: ai: Build smarter document workflows: What’s new in Azure Content Understanding at Build 2026
item.description:
Azure Content Understanding in Foundry Tools is described as a comprehensive content AI service for ingesting documents, audio, images, and video and extracting key information for grounded generative AI and agentic solutions. It combines Azure Document Intelligence’s traditional AI with LLM-based content capabilities.

  • Source: Microsoft AI Foundry Blog

item.title: github: GPT-4.1 deprecated
item.description:
GitHub deprecated GPT-4.1 in all GitHub Copilot experiences on June 1, 2026. The affected surface areas include Copilot Chat, inline edits, ask and agent modes, and code completions.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Code-first observability for Foundry Agents in VS Code
item.description:
Microsoft Foundry adds code-first observability for agent developers in public preview via an observe skill in the Foundry Plugin for VS Code Copilot Chat. The feature lets developers run the evaluation-driven optimization loop from within the editor.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Agent kit for Azure Cosmos DB
item.description:
Azure Cosmos DB Agent Kit is now generally available. It adds Cosmos DB best practices to AI coding workflows for building database applications.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure SQL updates for early-June
item.description:
Azure SQL now supports using AES 256 symmetric keys to configure transparent data encryption (TDE) with customer-managed keys in Azure SQL Database. This update is in public preview and applies to early June 2026.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Secure, Modern Access to Azure Files on macOS with MS Entra ID
item.description:
Azure Files now supports macOS access with Microsoft Entra ID authentication in public preview. This adds identity-based, secure access to file shares for cross-platform teams.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: File share centric management model (Microsoft.FileShares) for Azure Files
item.description:
The file share-centric management model for Azure Files is now generally available for NFS 4.1 shares on SSD storage. It uses the Microsoft.FileShares resource provider and makes file shares top-level Azure resources.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In development] Preview: Azure Cobalt 200 Arm-based Dpsv7, Dplsv7, Epsv7, Mpsv4, and Lpsv5-series Virtual Machines
item.description:
Azure has previewed new Arm-based Cobalt 200 VM families: Dpsv7, Dplsv7, Epsv7, Mpsv4, and Lpsv5. They are positioned as improvements over the previous Cobalt 100 generation.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure API Management introduces a Unified Model API for multi-model AI applications.
item.description:
Azure API Management is adding a Unified Model API in public preview for multi-model AI applications. It aims to reduce integration complexity across LLM providers by standardizing API format, SDK use, and governance.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Unified Text Translation API in Azure AI Translator
item.description:
Azure AI Translator’s Unified Text Translation API is now generally available. It provides a single endpoint for multiple translation backends, including neural machine translation, large language model translation, small language model translation, and Adaptive custom translation.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure API Management Premium v2 and Standard v2 now support wildcard custom hostnames.
item.description:
Azure API Management Premium v2 and Standard v2 now support wildcard custom hostnames. This lets you use a wildcard domain and certificate for subdomains, reducing per-hostname setup and certificate management overhead.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Global PTU Reservations Are Now Region-Agnostic
item.description:
Global Provisioned Throughput (PTU) reservations are now region-agnostic, so one reservation can cover Global PTU deployments across multiple regions. This change is intended to improve reservation utilization and savings.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Backup for Cosmos DB
item.description:
Azure Backup for Cosmos DB is now in public preview. It adds vaulted backups for Cosmos DB accounts to support cyber-resiliency, compliance, and isolated backup storage.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Policy Coverage for Model Router in Foundry Models
item.description:
Azure Policy now supports centralized governance for deployments that use the model router in Foundry Models. This lets organizations define and enforce routing standards to align model selection with security, compliance, and operational requirements.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Voice Live integration with Microsoft Foundry Agent Service
item.description:
Microsoft Foundry Agent Service is now generally available for Voice Live integration. Developers can connect real-time speech-to-text and text-to-speech to a Foundry Agent without building a custom audio pipeline.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In development] Private Preview: multiparty analytics with Azure Confidential Clean Rooms
item.description:
Microsoft is previewing multiparty analytics in Azure Confidential Clean Rooms, a fully managed service for securely analyzing privacy-sensitive datasets with partners using Apache Spark-based big data tools.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In development] Private Preview: Storage optimized Lasv5 and Laosv5 Azure VM series
item.description:
Lasv5 and Laosv5 storage-optimized Azure VM series are now in private preview. They use 5th Gen AMD EPYC (Turin) processors and target storage-intensive workloads, with Lasv5 focused on high disk capacity, throughput, and I/O.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Automatic OS Image Upgrades for VMSS Flex
item.description:
Automatic OS image upgrades are now in public preview for Virtual Machine Scale Sets (VMSS) using Flexible Orchestration. The feature extends automated fleet-wide image upgrades to VMSS Flex to reduce manual update work.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public preview: Azure Linux 4.0 for Azure Virtual Machines and VM Scale Sets
item.description:
Azure Linux 4.0 is in public preview for Azure Virtual Machines and VM Scale Sets. It introduces a newer platform stack, including Linux kernel 6.18 LTS and the dnf5 package manager.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Azure Container Linux (ACL) now generally available on Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS)
item.description:
Azure Container Linux (ACL) is now generally available for Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) node pools. It provides a minimal, immutable, container-focused host OS based on Flatcar Container Linux and Azure Linux RPM packages.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Event Grid - MQTT v5 Subscription Identifier
item.description:
Azure Event Grid Standard Namespace now generally supports MQTT v5 subscription identifiers. MQTT subscriptions can include an identifier that is delivered with incoming messages for immediate event routing.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Sphere OS version 26.06 is now available
item.description:
Azure Sphere OS version 26.06 is now available in the Retail feed. This release updates the OS only; there is no SDK update.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Dynamic threshold for Log search alerts
item.description:
General availability of Dynamic threshold for Log search alerts. Dynamic thresholds automatically calculate appropriate alert thresholds, reducing the need to set a fixed threshold manually.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Github Copilot modernization
item.description:
GitHub Copilot modernization agent is now generally available. It provides coordinated orchestration from estate to code to help scale application assessments and upgrades across an app portfolio.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: OpenTelemetry Metrics, Visualizations, and Enhanced Monitoring in Azure Monitor for Azure VMs and Arc Servers
item.description:
Azure Monitor for Azure VMs and Arc Servers now generally available with OpenTelemetry metrics and visualizations, providing a unified monitoring experience. The release consolidates key monitoring capabilities into a single experience.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Rolling updates in Flex Consumption
item.description:
Rolling updates are now generally available in Azure Functions Flex Consumption. They allow code and configuration changes to be applied without force-restarting all instances, enabling zero-downtime deployments with a simple configuration change.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: commandline: Introducing Command Line, and the new rules for builders
item.description:
Microsoft introduces a new “rules for builders” framing around agentic AI development, saying the traditional software development lifecycle is no longer sufficient. The post positions the guidance as a shift in workflows and mindset for building with AI agents.

  • Source: Microsoft Command Line Blog

item.title: commandline: Microsoft Scout: From personal project to enterprise-ready personal agent
item.description:
Microsoft Scout is described as an always-on personal AI agent that moved from a personal project into an enterprise-ready, secure product. The article is a discussion with Omar Shahine and Jakob Werner about the design and build process.

  • Source: Microsoft Command Line Blog

item.title: commandline: The agent optimization loop and how we built it in Foundry
item.description:
The article describes Microsoft’s approach to agent optimization in Foundry, reframing the problem to identify the factors that matter most and implementing a new optimization loop. The provided excerpt is high level and does not include technical details.

  • Source: Microsoft Command Line Blog

item.title: commandline: Grounding at scale: Engineering the retrieval system for the agentic web
item.description:
Microsoft introduced Web IQ, a new grounding system for the agentic web. The note is brief and does not include implementation details or feature specifics.

  • Source: Microsoft Command Line Blog

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Advanced filtering for DiskANN vector search in Azure HorizonDB
item.description:
Advanced filtering for DiskANN vector search in Azure HorizonDB is now in public preview. It lets you combine vector similarity search with metadata filters such as category, date range, price, or tenant ID.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Agent Memory Toolkit for Azure Cosmos DB
item.description:
Azure Cosmos DB now has a public preview of the Agent Memory Toolkit, a code-first toolkit for building agent memory systems. It is intended to support scalable, durable, and cost-efficient memory for agentic applications.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Agentic Retrieval Toolkit for Azure Cosmos DB
item.description:
Azure Cosmos DB now has a public preview of the Agentic Retrieval Toolkit. It is aimed at building advanced RAG applications for GraphRAG-style and multi-hop reasoning use cases.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Anyscale on Azure
item.description:
Anyscale on Azure is now in public preview. It provides a managed Ray platform for running Python workloads at scale on Azure.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Container Apps Sandboxes
item.description:
Azure Container Apps Sandboxes is in public preview. It is positioned for workloads that need to run untrusted code, retain session state, and absorb bursty demand.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Cosmos DB distributed transactions
item.description:
Azure Cosmos DB now has a public preview for distributed transactions. You can perform atomic operations across items and partitions, with all changes committed together or rolled back on failure.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Database PostgreSQL - Flexible Server cross-tenant customer-managed keys
item.description:
Azure Database for PostgreSQL - Flexible Server now supports cross-tenant customer-managed keys (CMK) in public preview. This lets you encrypt data at rest with an Azure Key Vault key stored in a different Microsoft Entra tenant than the database tenant.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Databricks Lakebase branching with GitHub Copilot agent mode
item.description:
Azure Databricks Lakebase now supports creating copy-on-write branches of a production database with a single command, then connecting GitHub Copilot agent mode to the branch endpoint for debugging AI apps and agents against production data. The feature is in public preview.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Databricks workspace-wide Genie MCP for Microsoft Copilot Studio
item.description:
Azure Databricks added a workspace-wide Genie MCP public preview endpoint for Microsoft Copilot Studio. It lets any Copilot Studio agent send natural-language queries through one endpoint that auto-routes across connected Genie spaces and Unity Catalog assets.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Functions now includes managed connectors to integrate with 1400+ of your favorite systems
item.description:
Azure Functions Public Preview now supports managed connectors as first-class triggers and operations. This brings access to the same 1,400+ connector ecosystem used by Logic Apps and Power Platform for integrating with external systems.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure HorizonDB
item.description:
Azure HorizonDB is in public preview as a PostgreSQL-compatible database service. The preview highlights autoscaling storage up to 128 TB and claimed performance improvements over self-managed PostgreSQL.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Logic Apps Automation SKU for agentic business process automation.
item.description:
Azure Logic Apps Automation SKU is in public preview. It is positioned for building agentic business process automation by combining workflows, APIs, and AI agents in one service.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Logic Apps Codeful Workflows with Logic Apps Standard SDK.
item.description:
Azure Logic Apps Standard SDK now has a public preview for codeful workflows, letting teams define workflows in code while using managed orchestration and enterprise connectors. The update targets integration and automation scenarios that want code-based development with cloud-scale operations.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Logic Apps now enables developers to invoke Microsoft Foundry Agents directly from workflows.
item.description:
Azure Logic Apps public preview now lets developers invoke Microsoft Foundry Agents directly from workflows. The update is intended to reduce the gap between agent development in Azure AI Foundry and process automation in Logic Apps.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure SQL Database provisioning in the MSSQL extension
item.description:
The MSSQL extension for Visual Studio Code now supports Azure SQL Database provisioning in public preview. It starts with the free tier and lets you create and connect to a managed cloud database directly from the editor.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Benchmark evaluations for fine-tuned models in Microsoft Foundry
item.description:
Microsoft Foundry now offers public preview benchmark evaluations for fine-tuned models. The feature is aimed at comparing model performance on tasks such as reasoning, math, and domain knowledge, where leaderboard results may not reflect real-world behavior.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: BM25 full-text search in Azure HorizonDB
item.description:
BM25 full-text search is now in public preview for Azure HorizonDB. The feature provides production-style keyword search with BM25 relevance ranking and runs entirely inside the database.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Change streams (multi-shard) in Azure DocumentDB
item.description:
Azure DocumentDB change streams are now in public preview with multi-node support. The feature lets you capture live data changes using controlled polling for real-time, event-driven applications.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: CLI and SDK support for Deployment Templates
item.description:
Azure Machine Learning deployment templates now support management through the CLI and SDK in public preview. Platform teams can create, update, delete, and manage templates from infrastructure-as-code pipelines instead of using only the Azure portal.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public preview: Connector Namespace
item.description:
Azure has introduced Connector Namespace in public preview. It is a managed service intended to simplify app integration with SaaS and line-of-business systems by providing pre-built connectors instead of custom API client code.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Content Understanding NextGen in Microsoft Foundry
item.description:
Microsoft Foundry now includes Content Understanding in the portal as a public preview, so users no longer need to switch to the standalone Content Understanding Studio for common tasks. The preview also adds Read and Layout playgrounds in Foundry for OCR text extraction.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Copilot-assisted coding & agent skills to build, deploy, and run Azure Functions faster in VS Code, CLI, and azd
item.description:
